title: About
intro: My research bridges theoretical quantum control and quantum simulation of open and closed quantum systems.
body: I develop advanced control protocols for open quantum systems, including path-integral quantum control (PiQC) algorithms and stochastic optimal control frameworks. My work connects quantum measurement dynamics to double-bracket gradient flows, enabling feedback-driven quantum state preparation with exponential convergence guarantees.

I have deep expertise in Lindblad master equations, quantum trajectories, and the mathematical foundations of continuous measurement processes (Stratonovich and Itô formulations), with new geometric interpretations of wavefunction collapse as coarse-grained gradient flows on unitary orbits.

My research on quantum computing theory and algorithms critically analyzes quantum annealing speedup prospects and develops practical ground-state preparation methods on noisy quantum hardware, addressing decoherence, measurement backaction, and noise cancellation.

I maintain production-level scientific software, including reduced-order modeling tools (arby) and quantum control implementations (PiQC) in MATLAB and Python, and I work with machine learning frameworks such as PyTorch and Keras for reduced-order modeling and quantum applications.
